issues.description: Agent used 5 figma_screenshot calls and 3 figma_execute calls for a simple button creation, exceeding the cap (figma_screenshot > 2). The agent struggled to locate the created element on the viewport and entered a zoom/scroll retry loop, inflating duration to 89s (borderline on the 90s cap). This wastes tokens and leaves the user waiting with no clear progress indicator during the retry attempts.

Context tokens jumped from 19K to 41K in a single turn due to excessive tool calls (9 tools in one turn). The redundant screenshots and execute calls bloat the context window unnecessarily, reducing headroom for subsequent conversation turns.

The agent response says 'it's likely positioned off the visible viewport' and then enters multiple screenshot-execute cycles to find and zoom to the button. The intermediate uncertainty ('likely positioned off') is confusing to the user -- the agent should either create the element at a known viewport position or navigate there directly without narrating its confusion.

The screenshot count in assertions shows 7 cumulative screenshots at this point, indicating the inline screenshot from step 4's excessive calls persists in the chat. While step 6 itself was efficient (only figma_set_fills + figma_screenshot), the chat scrollback shows accumulated visual noise from step 4's retry loop.

Judge triggered (triggeredTotal delta = 1) despite the test expecting it to be disabled. The metric_growth assertion failed on both sub-checks: judge.triggeredTotal was not 0 growth, and judge.skippedByReason['disabled'] did not increment. This is a known runner limitation (cannot perform the manual DOM toggle to disable the judge), but from a UX perspective the judge toggle three-state cycle is not testable by automation, indicating the toggle UX may be too subtle for users as well.

The judge verdict card was not visible when the screenshot was captured -- the dom_visible assertion on '.judge-verdict-card' failed. The screenshot shows 'Quality check running...' spinner still active at capture time. For a 122s turn with 20 tool calls, the judge evaluation did not complete before the runner's settle window. Users may see a spinning state with no resolution if they scroll away or switch tabs.

The agent called 20 tools in a single turn (5 task_create, 8 task_update, figma_design_system, figma_setup_tokens, figma_render_jsx, figma_get_file_data, figma_batch_bind_variable, figma_screenshot). While thorough, the task_create/task_update calls are internal bookkeeping that produce tool cards visible in the chat. The user sees a long stream of opaque 'task_update' cards with no indication of what each update means, degrading feedback clarity.

Quality check shows '0/121 items' in the verdict card footer, which is an opaque metric for the user. It is unclear what '121 items' refers to or why 0 of them have issues. A more descriptive summary (e.g., 'All checks passed') would be clearer.
